title: orthaea hispida
intro: Orthaea hispida, also known as the Queen of the Andes, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, specifically in the Andes mountains. It is a type of orchid and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Orthaea hispida include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions like acne and wounds. Orthaea hispida can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
